description: In November 1994, Norway held its second referendum on joining the European Union. Despite strong support from the ruling Labour Party and business elites, the 'No' campaign centered on sovereignty, fishing rights, and agricultural protectionism prevailed with 52.2% of the vote.

key_events.description: Norway concludes favorable accession negotiations in Brussels.
The 'No to EU' organization gains massive momentum in rural municipalities.
Former Prime Minister Gro Harlem Brundtland intensifies warnings about economic isolation.
Final TV debate highlights fears regarding the loss of national control over oil and fish resources.

calibration_notes: Ideal for calibrating against scenarios where institutional backing fails to overcome grassroots nationalist sentiment and economic sovereignty concerns.
